executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with 103 organizations compromised in a single day, April 15, 2026. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (hit by two separate groups), Markham Stouffville Hospital, Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, and the Municipality of North Perth. The Qilin ransomware group alone claimed 20 victims, while Clop gang hit 14 organizations and Play ransomware compromised 9. Critical infrastructure sectors affected include healthcare, education, public sector services, and financial services—posing immediate risks to Canadian citizens' personal data and essential services.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada experienced a massive coordinated ransomware wave on April 15, 2026, affecting 103 organizations across all provinces and sectors. Critical infrastructure was heavily targeted including healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital, Peterborough Public Health, BioPharma Services), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Collège Supérieur De Montréal, Teccart), public sector (Municipality of North Perth, Workers Health & Safety Centre), and telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network). No CCCS advisories were issued in the analyzed timeframe.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose was hit by two separate ransomware groups (CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters) on the same day. Active Green + Ross and Kids & Company were also compromised.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered a database leak via Everest ransomware. Morison Insurance Brokers was compromised by Sinobi ransomware. Multiple accounting firms were hit including Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, and SMT Lawyers.

report_2w.executive_summary: Google has patched an actively exploited zero-day vulnerability in Chrome (CVE-2026-5281) requiring immediate updates across all Canadian organizations. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued an alert for a critical Fortinet FortiClientEMS vulnerability (CVE-2026-35616). FBI and Indonesian authorities dismantled W3LLSTORE, a major phishing marketplace linked to $20 million in fraud. ShinyHunters ransomware group continues its spree, targeting Rockstar Games, Mytheresa, Marcus & Millichap, and Abrigo via SaaS supply chain compromises, primarily through stolen Snowflake credentials. Iranian-affiliated threat actors are actively targeting U.S. critical infrastructure PLCs, with separate claims of a 375TB breach of Lockheed Martin including alleged F-35 blueprints.

items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Medium

items.description: Heap-use-after-free vulnerability in HDF5 h5dump helper
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High (CVSS 7.8)

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Rockstar Games, Mytheresa, Marcus & Millichap Inc., Abrigo Inc., Hallmark
items.ttps: T1199 (Trusted Relationship - SaaS supply chain), T1078 (Valid Accounts - stolen Snowflake credentials), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact)

items.name: UNC1069
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise - software supply chain), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter), leveraging WAVESHAPER.V2 malware

items.name: Iranian-affiliated hackers (APT Iran)
items.targets: U.S. critical infrastructure PLCs (Rockwell), Lockheed Martin (alleged)
items.ttps: T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information), T1200 (Hardware Additions - PLC targeting), T1567 (Exfiltration - alleged 375TB including F-35 blueprints)

items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1598 (Phishing for Information), T1078 (Valid Accounts via social engineering)

items.ttps: T1071 (Application Layer Protocol), T1105 (Ingress Tool Transfer)

items.name: QuasarRAT operators
items.ttps: T1071 (Application Layer Protocol), T1219 (Remote Access Software)

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-007 warning of a vulnerability in Fortinet FortiClientEMS (CVE-2026-35616). The Bank of Canada and major Canadian lenders held emergency meetings to discuss cybersecurity risks posed by Anthropic's new AI model. Canada's military chief emphasized preparation for large-scale conflicts, highlighting national security concerns.
canada_landscape.financial: Bank of Canada and major Canadian lenders (unspecified banks) met to discuss cybersecurity risks from Anthropic AI model 'Mythos'
